raw_text:
```json
{
  "distinct_claims": [
    "California's housing crisis will not wait for the state budget to improve.",
    "Without the housing bond, the affordable housing pipeline will stall.",
    "A $10 billion bond would provide financial stability to many of the state’s core housing and preservation programs.",
    "Asm. Wicks’ bond would provide the funds to produce, preserve, and/or retrofit more than 120,000 affordable homes.",
    "Collectively, these projects will result in tens of thousands of high-paying construction jobs.",
    "Californians must earn $81,191 per year to afford the average two-bedroom apartment and avoid being rent-burdened.",
    "California needs to produce an additional 2.5 million housing units, including 1.2 million for lower-income households, to meet the state’s unmet housing needs.",
    "Resources from California's last housing bond, the $3 billion Veterans and Affordable Housing Bond Act of 2018, will be largely allocated by the end of 2024.",
    "The last round of funding for the Multi-family Housing Program was oversubscribed 10:1.",
    "AB 1657 will help ensure that California continues to make progress towards a future of housing stability and shared prosperity."
  ]
}
```
